era: Indie Pop. E=5 -- Second indie pop. Northgate enters. PopLlama continues. Since 1982. Scott McCaughey. R.E.M. touring member. v1.49.333.

members.name: Scott McCaughey
members.note: Founder. R.E.M. touring member (1994-2011). The Minus 5 with Peter Buck. PopLlama founder.

members.name: Jim Sangster
members.note: Longtime member. Also in The Minus 5.

associated_musicians.name: R.E.M.
associated_musicians.relationship: McCaughey touring member 1994-2011. Guitar and keyboards.

associated_musicians.name: The Minus 5
associated_musicians.relationship: McCaughey/Peter Buck collaborative project. Multiple albums.

associated_musicians.name: The Posies
associated_musicians.relationship: Fellow PopLlama band. Degree 191. Shared scene and label.

theory_nodes.concept: Northgate enters as new neighborhood. Suburban, unpretentious, anti-scene. Fourth new location in four degrees (189-192).
examples: Four new locations in four degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: McCaughey as network node -- connects PopLlama/Seattle indie to R.E.M./Athens to global alt-rock. Collaborations with Peter Buck, Robyn Hitchcock, and others.
theory_nodes.description: McCaughey as network node. Cross-scene connector.

theory_nodes.concept: PopLlama ecosystem -- two cornerstone bands in consecutive degrees: The Posies (191) and YFF (192). Seattle's 'other' label. Pop-oriented guitar music.
examples: Pop vs grunge. PopLlama vs Sub Pop.

cross_references.relationship: POPLLAMA CONSECUTIVE! Both on PopLlama. Power pop (191) to indie pop (192). Label's identity established.
SUB POP VS POPLLAMA! Three degrees apart. Grunge vs pop. Two labels, two aesthetics. Seattle's dual identity.
THE ENDURING! YFF since 1982 (44 years). Gray Whale = longest mammal migration. Both defined by persistence. Total E=10.
